name: Hispania's Crystal Trail
name_original: Ruinas romanas de Ercávica – circular desde Cañaveruelas por Alcohujate
description: Explore Roman ruins and reservoir views on this easy 13.7-mile mountain bike loop from Cañaveruelas, passing Alcohujate.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cañaveruelas offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the province of Cuenca, Castilla-La Mancha. The region is characterized by hills and mountains covered with forests, particularly around the Buendía Reservoir and the dramatic Hoz del Guadiela gorge. This area provides varied terrain, from challenging ascents to paths along water bodies, making it suitable for different mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Cañaveruelas The most popular mountain bike trail is Sacedón - Córcoles – circular por La Isabela y embalse de Entrepeñas, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Entrepeñas reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Entrepeñas Reservoir Shoreline Trail, a moderate 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path. This route follows the scenic shoreline of the reservoir, offering varied views. Local mountain bikers also love the Hispania's Crystal Trail, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Cañaveruelas is defined by reservoirs, gorges, and forested hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cañaveruelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cañaveruelas?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails documented in this guide around Cañaveruelas, offering a range of experiences from easy to difficult. The broader Cuenca province, accessible from Cañaveruelas, features hundreds of kilometers of trails.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Cañaveruelas?
A: You can expect diverse terrain, including hills and mountains covered with dense forests of oak and pine, particularly around the Buendía Reservoir and the dramatic Hoz del Guadiela gorge. Trails can range from challenging ascents to more flowing paths along water bodies, with some featuring technical elements like berms, drops, and rock gardens.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are easy trails available. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018000087/hispania-s-crystal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hispania's Crystal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.0 km route that can be completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes, leading through varied terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic views or pass by natural landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the Cañaveruelas area offer panoramic views of gorges, river systems, and geological formations. The region is known for its stunning natural beauty, including the Buendía Reservoir and the impressive Hoz del Guadiela. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; along some routes.

Q: Can I find loop trails for mountain biking around Cañaveruelas?
A: Yes, several loop trails are available. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003315446/sacedon-corcoles-circular-por-la-isabela-y-embalse-de-entrepenas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacedón - Córcoles – circular por La Isabela y embalse de Entrepeñas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.1 km route exploring the area around the Entrepeñas reservoir.

Q: What are some popular mountain bike routes that follow the shoreline of the reservoirs?
A: The region is well-known for its reservoirs. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003555010/entrepenas-reservoir-shoreline-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir Shoreline Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 39.0 km path that offers varied views along the water's edge.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that are particularly challenging for advanced riders?
A: Yes, the guide includes at least one difficult route. The broader Serranía de Cuenca, accessible from Cañaveruelas, is known for its mountainous terrain and significant slopes, offering challenging ascents and technical elements for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cañaveruelas?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: What kind of attractions can I find near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the reservoirs and gorges, you can find various attractions. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Guadiela River&lt;/a&gt;. These offer interesting stops along your ride.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that are less crowded?
A: While specific crowd levels vary, the extensive network of trails in the Serranía de Cuenca, accessible from Cañaveruelas, means you can often find routes that offer a sense of solitude. Exploring some of the less-trafficked paths away from the main reservoir areas can lead to a more peaceful experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cañaveruelas?
A: Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the serene reservoir shorelines to the dramatic gorges and forested hills. The variety of routes, catering to different skill levels, and the immersive natural experience with potential wildlife sightings, are frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer views of the Entrepeñas Dam?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide views of the dam. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19812003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Entrepeñas Dam – Royal Site of La Isabela loop from Sacedón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 49.6 km route that includes views of this significant landmark.
tips: Small town in the interior of Cuenca with some services. The surrounding environment is very picturesque and is located near the Buendía reservoir and the Roman ruins of Ercávica.
The ruins of the Roman city of Ercávica from the year 179 B.C. approximately. Free visit until June 30, 2023 (normal entry 2 eur). There is no toilet.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route from Cañaveruelas by Alcohujate, passing the Roman City of Ercávica, is generally considered easy. While the Cuenca region offers varied terrain, this specific trail is suitable for most riders, though you might encounter a mix of well-maintained tracks, gravel paths, and some stony sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse landscape typical of the Serranía de Cuenca. You'll ride on a mix of well-maintained tracks, forest roads, and both paved and unpaved country roads. Expect varied topography including rolling hills, agricultural fields, and sections offering scenic views of the Roman City of Ercávica and the Buendía reservoir.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or natural landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The primary highlight is the Roman City of Ercávica, offering a unique blend of history and outdoor activity. You'll also pass through the settlement of Alcohujate and enjoy picturesque views of the Buendía reservoir and the natural beauty of the Cuenca province, characterized by pine forests and canyons.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact trailhead are not provided, it's generally advisable to look for parking in or near Cañaveruelas, as it's the starting and ending point of this circular route. Look for public parking areas within the village or designated spots near the main access roads.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas like the Serranía de Cuenca are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through settlements like Alcohujate, near archaeological sites like the Roman City of Ercávica, or in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is usually milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Roman ruins?
faqs.answer: No specific permits are generally required for mountain biking on public trails in this region. Access to the Roman City of Ercávica archaeological site itself may have specific opening hours or a small entrance fee, so it's advisable to check their official website if you plan to explore the ruins in depth.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other established paths. Approximately 22% of the route follows the Ruta del Cristal de Hispania, and about 5% overlaps with the Camino de La Isabela. This offers a chance to experience parts of these longer trails within your ride.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach Cañaveruelas or Alcohujate?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to smaller villages like Cañaveruelas and Alcohujate in rural Cuenca can be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules in advance, as services might be infrequent, especially on weekends. Driving is often the most convenient way to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay overnight near the trail?
faqs.answer: Cañaveruelas and nearby Alcohujate are small villages, but you may find local bars, restaurants, or rural accommodation options. It's a good idea to check availability and opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak season, as services can be limited.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this easy mountain bike route, essential items include plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and appropriate cycling attire. Given the varied terrain, a helmet is a must, and comfortable cycling shoes are recommended. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views!
